content.text:
<user_query>
I just refactored to use t3-env and now I get an error saying invalid environment variables. Lets see if there's a documented way to use t3-env to get a more specific error message about which environment variable exactly is having an issue
</user_query>
I need to look at their t3-env configuration and understand what's causing the error they're seeing, then check the documentation to find a more helpful solution.

The user needs more detailed error messages from their validation, and t3-env has an `onValidationError` callback that can provide field-level information from the underlying Zod errors. Let me look into what the default behavior actually outputs and whether they're getting the full error details or just a generic message.

input.description: find t3 env types in monorepo

input.name: t3-env detailed errors
input.overview: Add a custom `onValidationError` handler to `src/env.ts` so t3-env surfaces the specific variable name(s) and reason in the thrown error, instead of the generic "Invalid environment variables".

todos.content: Add custom onValidationError to apps/org-next/src/env.ts that formats each issue's path and message into the thrown error
Re-run the app and confirm the error now identifies the specific failing variable

input.description: Verify env error with invalid values

content.text: Verified. The error now clearly identifies each invalid variable and the specific reason, both in the `console.error` output and in the thrown `Error` message itself.

todos.content: Re-run the app and confirm the error now identifies the specific failing variable

content.text:
Both tasks complete.

## Summary

Added a custom `onValidationError` handler to [apps/org-next/src/env.ts](apps/org-next/src/env.ts) that formats each Standard Schema issue as `VAR_NAME: message` and includes them in both the `console.error` log and the thrown `Error`.

Verification with bad values for `API_HOSTNAME` and `SESSION_SECRET` produced:

```
Invalid environment variables:
  - API_HOSTNAME: Invalid URL
  - SESSION_SECRET: Invalid UUID
```

Typecheck passes (`pnpm typecheck`). Re-run your app now and the error will tell you exactly which env var is misconfigured.
